articles.description: DMK's Paul Manoj Pandian secured victory in the Alangulam Assembly election by a margin of 7,798 votes. The constituency, located in Tenkasi district, faces persistent issues including irrigation shortages, poor infrastructure, and limited healthcare and education facilities. Youth unemployment remains a significant concern for the underdeveloped region.

articles.title: Bahour election result 2026: DMK's R Senthil Kumar emerges as the winner with 10520 votes
articles.description: DMK's R Senthil Kumar has secured victory in the Bahour constituency, defeating T Thiagarajan by 2397 votes in the 2026 assembly elections. This agrarian region in southern Puducherry witnessed a remarkable 93.99% voter turnout by 7 pm. Bahour's electoral landscape has seen shifting loyalties, with Kumar's win marking a continuation of this trend.

articles.title: ‘Lotus Blooms In Bengal’: PM Modi, Amit Shah’s First Reaction After BJP’s Landslide Win
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi welcomed BJP’s massive lead in the West Bengal Assembly Elections, calling it a historic mandate and a victory of good governance. Home Minister Amit Shah said the results reflect people’s rejection of fear and appeasement politics. With BJP leading in over 195 seats, celebrations spread across Kolkata, beginning with jhalmuri gatherings and turning into a full Bengali feast at the Salt Lake headquarters. Party workers served fish curry, rice, and sweets to hundreds. The festive mood continues as counting remains underway, with the TMC trailing and final results yet to be officially declared.

articles.title: PM Modi wears traditional Bengali dhoti and kurta as BJP clean sweeps West Bengal for first time
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi donned a traditional Bengali dhoti at BJP headquarters following the party's decisive victory in West Bengal. The BJP is set to form its first government, ending the TMC's 15-year rule with a significant majority. This win signifies a major political shift, with the BJP's vote share increasing substantially.

articles.title: BENGAL’S POLITICAL EARTHQUAKE: BJP NEARS 200 SEATS TRINAMOOL TRAILS AS STATE DEMANDS CHANGE
articles.description: West Bengal is rewriting its political map. BJP is closing in on 200 seats in a 293-seat assembly, crossing the majority mark, while Trinamool Congress — which has ruled the state for over a decade — trails significantly. A record 92% voter turnout, returning migrant workers, first-time voters, and years of built-up anti-incumbency have all converged in one historic verdict. Mamata Banerjee holds her personal seat in Bhabanipur, but her party’s ground machinery has cracked across the state. North Bengal, Junglemahal, and even traditional TMC strongholds are shifting. This isn’t a sudden surge — it’s a slow tide that finally broke. West Bengal has seen political earthquakes before. This may be another one.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of harassing workers; assures win, asks not to fear
articles.description: Greetings to everyone. I urge the counting agents and candidates not to leave their assigned areas. Please do not leave the strong rooms where the vote counting is underway. This, I believe, was part of the BJP’s plan. I have been saying since yesterday that they will show their leads in the first two to three rounds, and our results will be reflected later. In several areas, after two to three rounds, vote counting at nearly 100 locations was stopped, and seven EVMs were reportedly found red-handed in Kalyani that were not identical. This appears to be a deliberate effort by the Central Government using central forces. They are allegedly attacking TMC workers everywhere, breaking offices, and forcibly taking control. They have been accused of manipulating votes in the name of SIR to win seats. However, there are at least 70 to 100 seats where we are leading, but those results are not being shown. This is completely false information being circulated. Earlier, such reports were being shared by DIC, but that changed yesterday. The Election Commission is acting according to its own decisions, with the support of central forces. Even our police are allegedly working under pressure, with their heads down. That is why I am telling counting agents and party workers not to lose heart. I have said before that we will win by sunset. Only three to four rounds have been completed so far, and the process goes up to 16 to 18 rounds. Wait and watch. We are with you—do not be afraid.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026: PMK Outperforms Expectations, Leads In 5 Seats, Fuels Kingmaker Buzz
articles.description:
Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026: The Pattali Makkal Katchi (PMK), led by Anbumani Ramadoss, is emerging as a notable player in the Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026, with counting trends showing the party ahead in five constituencies. Contesting 18 seats as part of an alliance, PMK’s performance is drawing attention not just for its numbers, but for its potential role in post-poll arithmetic.

Even as counting continues, political observers are closely watching whether PMK could turn into a decisive factor if no single party secures a clear majority.

Leads Across Key Constituencies

PMK has taken the lead in multiple constituencies, reflecting its concentrated influence in select regions:

  • Pennagaram (58): Selvam V. — 19,725 votes, margin 3,612
  • Dharmapuri (59): Sowmiya Anbumani — 46,080 votes, margin 6,817
  • Gingee (70): Ganeshkumar A. — 18,514 votes, margin 4,334
  • Vikravandi (75): Sivakumar C. — 15,690 votes, margin 547
  • Jayankondam (150): Vaithilingam G. — 41,388 votes, margin 9,693

These leads underline PMK’s ability to remain competitive despite contesting a limited number of seats.

Alliance Politics and Kingmaker Speculation

For the 2026 elections, PMK aligned with the AIADMK-BJP front. However, speculation continues that the party could play a pivotal role after results are declared. If Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), led by Vijay, falls short of a majority, PMK’s support could become crucial.

Anbumani Ramadoss has not ruled out backing TVK, keeping political options open and fuelling discussions around potential post-poll realignments.

ALSO READ: TVK Wave In Tamil Nadu; Vijay’s Party Leads In 100+ Seats In Historic Debut

The PMK

Founded on July 16, 1989, by S. Ramadoss, PMK traces its origins to the Vanniyar Sangam movement. The party has historically championed social justice and representation, particularly for the Vanniyar community, giving it a strong regional base.

This legacy continues to shape its electoral strategy, allowing it to maintain relevance even in a rapidly evolving political landscape.

Leadership Rift Casts Shadow

The election campaign unfolded against the backdrop of a significant internal conflict within PMK. A power struggle between founder S. Ramadoss and his son Anbumani Ramadoss escalated into a legal dispute over control of the party’s name, symbol, and organisational authority.

The disagreement, which intensified through 2025 and early 2026, centred on leadership autonomy and decision-making powers. The Madras High Court has directed that the matter be heard by a civil court after the Assembly elections, leaving the issue unresolved for now.

ALSO READ: Tamil Nadu Election Results: Who Is VS Babu, TVK Leader Behind Kolathur Upset Challenging Stalin?

A Small Party With Big Influence?

Despite internal challenges, PMK’s early leads suggest it could punch above its weight in this election. With a handful of seats but strategic importance, the party may find itself in a position to influence government formation if the final tally produces a hung Assembly.

articles.description:
Sainthia (SC) Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE:

Catch latest win-loss tally and vote counting updates from West Bengal Election 2026 here. Sainthia (SC) constituency number 289 of West Bengal, was won by Nilabati Saha in 2021 from TMC who secured 110572 votes. In 2021, the runner up candidate was, Piya Saha from BJP who secured 95329 votes. Winning margin for this seat in 2021 elections was 15243 votes.
Vote counting of Assembly elections 2026 will be held today, 4 May 2026 from 8 AM onwards. Catch latest updates on ABP Live for all details on vote counting, win loss tally and final seats won by each party.
